Home › The Degeneracy Power Law

The Degeneracy Power Law

The Degeneracy Power Law is the relationship \( P = K ho^\Gamma \) that describes how Degeneracy Pressure scales with density. In university-level astrophysics, this is "Polytropic Equation of State." The value of the exponent \( \Gamma \) determines the internal structure and Stability Threshold of White Dwarfs and Neutron Stars, reflecting the transition from Non-Relativistic to relativistic regimes.

1. The 5/3 Law: Non-Relativistic Stability

For particles moving much slower than the speed of light, the power law has an index \( \Gamma = 5/3 \). \[ P \propto ho^{5/3} \] This index is "Stiff" enough to guarantee a stable equilibrium. In the , a star governed by this law will always find a radius where its internal pressure balances its own weight, regardless of how much mass is added (up to a point). This describes the Total Dynamics of Brown Dwarfs and low-mass stellar remnants.

2. The 4/3 Law: The Relativistic Softening

When particles approach the Cosmic Speed Limit (\( c \)), the power law "Softens" to \( \Gamma = 4/3 \). \[ P \propto ho^{4/3} \] This softening occurs because as you add energy to a relativistic particle, its momentum increases but its speed cannot. This reduces the rate at which pressure builds up, leading to the Chandrasekhar Limit where gravity can finally "out-run" the Quantum Resistance.

3. Polytropes and the Lane-Emden Equation

A star following a power-law pressure is called a Polytrope. The density distribution \( ho(r) \) is solved using the Lane-Emden Equation.

  • n = 1.5 (\(\Gamma=5/3\)): Describes a stable, non-relativistic white dwarf.
  • n = 3.0 (\(\Gamma=4/3\)): Describes a star at the edge of collapse.
This mathematical framework links the subatomic Planck Constant to the 3D radius and Initial Mass of the star.

4. Adiabatic Index and Sound Speed

The power law index is related to the **Adiabatic Index** \( \gamma \). It determines the speed of sound in degenerate matter: \( c_s = \sqrt{\Gamma P / ho} \). In Neutron Stars, the sound speed can approach \( c \), testing the Epistemic Limit of causality. A power law with \( \Gamma \gt 3 \) would theoretically allow for faster-than-light sound, a violation that constrains our 4D Theoretical Physics models of the nuclear force.

5. Transitions in the History of the Universe

The power law has evolved as the universe expanded. In the Early Universe, the "Effective Power Law" of the primordial plasma dictated the Expansion History. The transition from radiation (\( \Gamma=4/3 \)) to matter (\( \Gamma=5/3 \)) dominance allowed for the first structures to cluster, showing that the "Exponent of Pressure" is the driver of cosmic growth.

6. Modified Gravity and the Exponent

In Modified Newtonian Dynamics (MOND) or General Relativity extensions, the effective power law might be altered by the curvature of the . Measuring the mass-radius relation of Neutron Stars (which follows the power law) is a precision test of gravity. Any deviation from the predicted \( \Gamma \) would indicate "New Physics" in the way matter curves the 4D vacuum energy.

Key Theoretical Identities

Interact with identities to explore their semantic depth.

  • The Gamma Factor [ Click to Expand Depth ]
    \[ P = K ho^{1 + 1/n} \]

    1. Physical Interpretation

    The Gamma Factor: The number that tells us if a star is "Hard" or "Soft" under pressure.

    2. Symmetry & Origin

    Great Expansion: Symmetry derivation pending.

    3. Limits & Boundary

    Great Expansion: Limiting case analysis pending.

  • Stable Rule [ Click to Expand Depth ]
    \[ \Gamma = \frac{d \ln P}{d \ln ho} \]

    1. Physical Interpretation

    Stable Rule: The math behind why most packed objects can support their own weight.

    2. Symmetry & Origin

    Great Expansion: Symmetry derivation pending.

    3. Limits & Boundary

    Great Expansion: Limiting case analysis pending.

  • The Danger Zone [ Click to Expand Depth ]
    \[ \frac{1}{\xi^2} \frac{d}{d\xi} (\xi^2 \frac{d heta}{d\xi}) = - heta^n \quad ( ext{Lane-Emden}) \]

    1. Physical Interpretation

    The Danger Zone: Why high-speed particles lose the ability to push back effectively.

    2. Symmetry & Origin

    Great Expansion: Symmetry derivation pending.

    3. Limits & Boundary

    Great Expansion: Limiting case analysis pending.

  • Lane-Emden Link [ Click to Expand Depth ]
    \[ c_s^2 = \frac{\partial P}{\partial ho} \]

    1. Physical Interpretation

    Lane-Emden Link: The set of equations used to draw the 3D internal map of a dead star.

    2. Symmetry & Origin

    Great Expansion: Symmetry derivation pending.

    3. Limits & Boundary

    Great Expansion: Limiting case analysis pending.
